title: [UI Bug] Navigation crash - Cannot read property navigate of undefined
description:
## Error
Uncaught Error: Cannot read property 'navigate' of undefined

## Severity
SYSTEMIC - affects nearly all navigation actions. Occurs repeatedly (Log 4 of 4, Log 5 of 5 observed).

## Call Stack (partial)
- loadModuleImplementation (require.js:238)
- _performTransitionSideEffects (Pressability.js:758)
- _receiveSignal (Pressability.js:697)
- responderEventHandlers.onResponderRelease

## Likely Cause
Something is calling navigate() when nav-ref is undefined. Possibilities:
1. A useEffect or subscription callback tries to navigate before NavigationContainer mounts
2. Hot reload invalidates the nav-ref but components still hold stale reference
3. Error boundary or toast component tries to navigate on error

## Reproduction
Almost any navigation action triggers this:
- Navigate to SessionList with empty params
- Navigate to CommandExecution
- General navigation flow

## Impact
Blocks normal app usage - error overlay appears frequently
close_reason: Closed

title: Draft message persistence
description:
iOS uses DraftManager to persist draft messages per-session to UserDefaults with 300ms debounce. RN drafts stored only in volatile app-db.

**iOS DraftManager Features:**
- Per-session draft storage (session ID -> text)
- Debounced writes to UserDefaults (300ms)
- save(), get(), clear(), cleanup() lifecycle
- Survives app restarts and background

**Current RN State:**
- Drafts in app-db under :ui :drafts
- Lost on app restart
- No debouncing on writes

**Implementation:**
1. Add draft persistence to AsyncStorage
2. Debounce writes (300ms) to reduce I/O
3. Load drafts on app init
4. Clear draft on message send
5. Cleanup stale drafts periodically

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add draft persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add draft save effect

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Draft text survives app restart
- [ ] Drafts debounced to reduce I/O
- [ ] Draft cleared after successful send
- [ ] Old drafts for deleted sessions cleaned up
close_reason: Closed

title: [UI Bug] Unsafe navigation calls - missing nil checks
description:
## Problem
Multiple views call .navigate on the navigation prop without checking if it exists.

## Affected Files
- directory_list.cljs: settings-button uses (.navigate navigation ...) without guard
- session_list.cljs: commands-button and render-item use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard  
- command_menu.cljs: command on-press handlers use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard

## Root Cause
The navigation prop can be undefined during:
- Hot reload (stale references)
- Component mounting race conditions
- Module loading

## Fix
Wrap all .navigate calls:
```clojure
(when navigation
  (.navigate navigation "ScreenName" params))
```

Or use the safe navigate! function from voice-code.views.core:
```clojure
(voice-code.views.core/navigate! "ScreenName" params)
```

## Related
This is the root cause of VCMOB-1i4 (navigation crash)
close_reason: Closed

title: Increment unread count for background session responses
description:
When a response arrives for a session that is not currently active (viewed), the unread count should be incremented. Currently ws/handle-response doesn't dispatch sessions/increment-unread for non-active sessions.

**Expected behavior:**
- When response received for session-id != active-session-id
- Dispatch [:sessions/increment-unread session-id]
- Unread badge will appear in session list

**Implementation:**
- In ws/handle-response, after confirming success
- Check if session-id != active-session-id  
- If so, dispatch sessions/increment-unread event
- The sessions/clear-unread event should be called when user enters the conversation view

Note: This is separate from the directory-level unread badge (VCMOB-agk) which aggregates counts.

## Description

## iOS Reference
- DirectoryListView uses SwiftUI's `.onMove` modifier
- Method: `reorderPriorityQueue(from source: IndexSet, to destination: Int)`
- Calculates neighbor priorityOrder values and positions new item between them
- Full CoreData model support for priorityOrder field

## Current RN State
- Priority queue displays in directory_list.cljs (lines ~200+)
- Shows visual indicators (colored backgrounds by priority level)
- No drag capability (code comment notes this)
- Quote from code: 'Note: Drag-to-reorder would require react-native-draggable-flatlist'

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add react-native-draggable-flatlist or similar library
- [ ] Implement drag-to-reorder for priority queue section
- [ ] Update priorityOrder values on reorder
- [ ] Persist reordering to backend
- [ ] Maintain visual consistency with iOS (priority-level tinting)

## Technical Notes
- Library options: react-native-draggable-flatlist, react-native-sortable-list
- Need to calculate new priorityOrder between neighbors (iOS uses midpoint strategy)
- May need WebSocket message to notify backend of priority changes

title: Auto-speak Claude responses when enabled

## Summary
Message bubbles appear empty in the conversation view when they contain non-text content like tool calls or thinking steps. The bubbles render with correct styling and timestamps but no visible content.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a conversation with Claude
2. Ask something that triggers tool calls or thinking
3. Observe empty gray (assistant) and blue (user) bubbles in the conversation

## Expected Behavior
- Tool calls should show a collapsed/summary view (e.g., 'Reading file...', 'Running command...')
- Thinking steps could show '[Thinking...]' or be hidden entirely
- Empty messages should not render as bubbles at all

## Actual Behavior
Empty bubbles render with timestamps but no content, creating a confusing UI with dead space.

## Technical Notes
- Backend may be sending messages with empty text but other content (tool_use, thinking blocks)
- Frontend message rendering may not handle these content types
- Consider filtering out messages with no displayable content

title: [UI Bug] SessionList shows 'No Sessions' despite data being available in subscription
description:
## Description
SessionList and Conversation views show empty state despite subscriptions containing data.

## Affected Views
1. **SessionList** - Shows 'No Sessions' when subscription has 20 sessions
2. **Conversation** - Shows 'Start a Conversation' when subscription has 54 messages

## Root Cause
Likely Reagent reactivity issue with `r/reactify-component` wrapper used for React Navigation screens. The reactive context may not be properly established when components are created through React Navigation.

## Additional Finding
Messages in the subscription have nil `:text` and `:role` fields - possible data parsing issue from WebSocket message handling.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Authenticate via REPL
2. Navigate to SessionList or Conversation
3. Observe empty state despite subscription returning data

## Technical Analysis
```clojure
;; Subscription returns correct data:
@(rf/subscribe [:sessions/for-directory "/path"]) ;; => 20 sessions
@(rf/subscribe [:messages/for-session id]) ;; => 54 messages

;; But components check (empty? sessions) or (empty? messages) => true
```

## Impact
P1 - Core functionality broken. Users cannot see sessions or messages.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern. The root cause was subscriptions being read in the outer function scope of components wrapped with r/reactify-component for React Navigation. Moving subscriptions into the inner render function establishes proper Reagent reactive context.

title: [UI Bug] DirectoryList session count not displayed - clj->js key conversion issue
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the app
2. View the Projects (DirectoryList) screen
3. Observe each directory item shows 'sessions' without a number

## Expected Behavior
Each directory item should show the session count, e.g., '3 sessions' or '1 session'

## Actual Behavior
Each directory item just shows 'sessions' with no count

## Screenshot
/tmp/test-screenshot-1.png

## Root Cause
In directory_list.cljs, the render-item function uses:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
```

But `clj->js` converts `{:session-count 5}` to JavaScript object with key `"session-count"` (preserves kebab-case as string), NOT `sessionCount`.

## Fix
Change the property access from:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
:last-modified (.-lastModified item)
```
to:
```clojure
:session-count (aget item "session-count")
:last-modified (aget item "last-modified")
```

Or use `:keywordize-keys` option when converting.

## Additional Context
- File: frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs
- Function: directory-list-view, render-item callback
- Same issue likely affects last-modified display
close_reason: Closed

title: [UI Bug] Screen frozen on CommandExecution - navigation/auth state changes don't trigger re-render
description:
## Description
The UI becomes completely frozen on the CommandExecution screen and does not respond to any navigation or authentication state changes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. App is showing CommandExecution screen (from previous command)
2. Authenticate via REPL
3. Attempt to navigate to any screen via `navigate!` or `.reset`
4. Navigation state changes (verified via `.getState`) but UI doesn't update

## Observed Behavior
- Navigation state shows `{:index 2, :routes [DirectoryList, Settings, DirectoryList]}`
- UI continues to show CommandExecution screen (not in routes array!)
- Setting `authenticated? false` doesn't show auth-view
- `.reset` with new routes doesn't update display

## Expected Behavior
- Navigation should update displayed screen
- Auth state change should switch between auth-view and NavigationContainer

## Technical Details
- nav-ref.getState() returns correct state
- re-frame subscriptions return correct values
- React component tree not re-rendering
- re-frame warning visible: "no :event handler registered for: (...)"

## Impact
P0 - App unusable when this state occurs. Requires app restart to recover.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern (inner render function) so subscriptions are read inside a reactive context. This ensures React Navigation screens properly re-render when re-frame subscriptions change.

## Protocol (from STANDARDS.md)
```json
{"type": "message_ack", "message_id": "<unique-message-id>"}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Call :ws/send-message-ack from :ws/handle-response for successful responses
- [ ] Call :ws/send-message-ack from :ws/handle-replay for replayed messages
- [ ] Verify acks sent via network inspection

title: Fix backend restart silently failing when process started from different directory
description:
## Problem

The `make backend-restart` command silently fails when a backend server is already running but was started from a different working directory (or manually). The Makefile tracks PIDs in `.backend-pid`, but if the process was started elsewhere, the PID file doesn't exist or points to a dead process.

### Symptoms
- `make backend-restart` reports 'Backend server started (PID: XXXXX)'
- But the new process immediately exits because port 8080 is already bound
- Old backend continues running with stale code
- User thinks restart succeeded but changes aren't loaded

### Root Cause
- `start-server.sh` starts the backend with `nohup` in background
- If port 8080 is already bound, the new process fails silently
- The PID file gets updated with the new (now dead) PID
- No error is shown to the user

### Solution
1. Check if port 8080 is already bound before starting
2. If bound, fail with clear error message: 'Port 8080 already in use. Run make backend-stop-all first.'
3. Alternatively, add port check to `backend-run` target in Makefile

### Workaround
Use `pkill -f 'voice-code.server'` or `make backend-stop-all` before `make backend-run`
close_reason: Fixed in commit f1f863e - start-server.sh now detects port conflicts before starting and reads port from config.edn

title: Add input validation to resolve-command-id
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/resolve-command-id-validation.md

## Overview
Add defensive input validation to the `resolve-command-id` function in commands.clj to prevent malformed shell commands from being executed. The function currently accepts any string and can produce invalid commands for edge cases like nil, empty strings, or incomplete prefixes.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resolve-command-id` throws `ExceptionInfo` for `nil` input
2. `resolve-command-id` throws `ExceptionInfo` for empty/blank string input
3. `resolve-command-id` throws `ExceptionInfo` for `"git."` (no subcommand)
4. `resolve-command-id` throws `ExceptionInfo` for `"bd."` (no subcommand)
5. `resolve-command-id` logs resolved command at debug level
6. All existing command resolution behavior unchanged for valid inputs
7. All new tests pass
8. All existing tests continue to pass

title: Worktree beads isolation via BEADS_DB environment variable
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md

## Overview
Implement transparent, automatic isolation of beads databases per git worktree using the BEADS_DB environment variable. Each worktree gets a local .beads-local/ database that is automatically created on session start, eliminating noise from unrelated issues in the main repository.

## Key Components
1. New `voice-code.env` namespace with detection, initialization, and env building functions
2. Integration with `claude.clj` process execution
3. Integration with `commands.clj` process spawning
4. Worktree session creation in `server.clj`
5. Unit and integration tests
6. `.gitignore` update

## Acceptance Criteria
1. [ ] Worktrees are automatically detected by checking `git rev-parse --git-dir` output for `/worktrees/`
2. [ ] Detection results are cached per-directory to avoid repeated git calls
3. [ ] `.beads-local/local.db` is created when worktree session starts
4. [ ] `BEADS_DB` environment variable is set for all processes spawned in worktree directories
5. [ ] `bd` commands in worktrees see only worktree-specific issues
6. [ ] Main repository beads database is unaffected
7. [ ] `.beads-local/` is in `.gitignore`
8. [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
9. [ ] New unit tests cover detection, env building, and db initialization
close_reason: All implementation complete. Created voice-code.env namespace, integrated with claude.clj and commands.clj, updated worktree session creation to use local beads db, added .gitignore entry. All 281 backend tests and 12 iOS tests pass.

title: Create voice-code.env namespace with worktree detection
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md#api-design

## Context
This is the foundational task that creates the new `voice-code.env` namespace. All other integration tasks depend on this namespace existing. It provides the core functions for detecting worktrees, initializing local beads databases, and computing environment variables.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create new file `backend/src/voice_code/env.clj`
- [ ] Implement `worktree-cache` atom for memoization
- [ ] Implement `detect-worktree` function using `git rev-parse --git-dir`
- [ ] Implement `ensure-beads-local!` function to initialize `.beads-local/local.db`
- [ ] Implement `env-for-directory` function to return BEADS_DB env var map
- [ ] Use `shell/sh :env` merge pattern to preserve PATH, HOME, etc.

## Technical Approach
- Files to create: `backend/src/voice_code/env.clj`
- Dependencies: None (foundation task)

Key implementation details:
- `detect-worktree`: Check if `git rev-parse --git-dir` output contains `/worktrees/`
- `ensure-beads-local!`: Create `.beads-local/` dir, run `bd init --force` with BEADS_DB env var
- `env-for-directory`: Return `{"BEADS_DB" path}` for worktrees with existing db, `{}` otherwise

- [ ] Test `detect-worktree` caches results
- [ ] Test `ensure-beads-local!` creates database

- [ ] Test `ensure-beads-local!` preserves PATH in env
- [ ] Test `env-for-directory` returns correct map

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: Worktrees are automatically detected by checking `git rev-parse --git-dir` output for `/worktrees/`
- AC2: Detection results are cached per-directory to avoid repeated git calls

title: Add environment variable support to commands.clj process spawning
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md#integration-points

## Context
This task integrates the env namespace with shell command execution. When commands are run via the iOS Commands UI in a worktree, the BEADS_DB environment variable must be set so that `bd.ready`, `bd.list`, etc. use the local database.

## Requirements
- [ ] Modify `spawn-process` to call `env/env-for-directory`
- [ ] Use `ProcessBuilder.environment().putAll()` to add env vars
- [ ] Add require for `voice-code.env` namespace

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/commands.clj`
- Dependencies: Task "Create voice-code.env namespace" must complete first

Key changes:
```clojure
(let [env-vars (env/env-for-directory working-directory)
      pb (ProcessBuilder. ["bash" "-c" shell-command])
      _ (.directory pb (java.io.File. working-directory))
      _ (when (seq env-vars)
          (.putAll (.environment pb) env-vars))
      process (.start pb)]
  ...)
```

Note: ProcessBuilder.environment() returns a mutable map that inherits from parent process, so `.putAll` adds to (not replaces) the environment.

## Verification
- [ ] Existing commands.clj tests still pass
- [ ] Integration test: spawn-process in worktree passes BEADS_DB
- [ ] Manual: Run `bd.ready` command in worktree via iOS, verify sees local db

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC4: `BEADS_DB` environment variable is set for all processes spawned in worktree directories

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Add environment variable support to claude.clj"
close_reason: Added env require and modified spawn-process to call env/env-for-directory and pass vars via ProcessBuilder.environment().putAll(). All 9 available-commands tests pass.

title: Initialize beads local database in worktree session creation
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md#integration-points

## Context
This task ensures that when a new worktree session is created, the local beads database is initialized before any Claude prompts are sent. This is the setup step that makes the BEADS_DB environment variable useful.

## Requirements
- [ ] Call `env/ensure-beads-local!` after successful `create-worktree!`
- [ ] Handle failure case: send error to client if beads init fails
- [ ] Add require for `voice-code.env` namespace

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- Dependencies: Task "Create voice-code.env namespace" must complete first

Key changes in worktree session creation flow:
```clojure
(let [wt-result (worktree/create-worktree! parent-directory branch-name worktree-path)]
  (if (:success wt-result)
    (let [beads-result (env/ensure-beads-local! worktree-path sanitized-name)]
      (if (:success beads-result)
        ;; Continue with Claude invocation...
        (let [prompt (worktree/format-worktree-prompt ...)] ...)
        ;; Beads init failed
        (send-error! channel {:error (:error beads-result) ...})))
    ;; Worktree creation failed
    ...))
```

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: worktree creation calls ensure-beads-local!
- [ ] Unit test: beads init failure sends error to client
- [ ] Manual: Create worktree via iOS, verify `.beads-local/local.db` exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC3: `.beads-local/local.db` is created when worktree session starts

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Add environment variable support to claude.clj" and "Add environment variable support to commands.clj"
close_reason: Replaced worktree/init-beads! with env/ensure-beads-local! in server.clj worktree session creation. All 66 server tests pass.

title: SelectableText component for reliable text selection
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/selectable-text-component.md

## Overview
Create a cross-platform SelectableText component using UITextView (iOS) and NSTextView (macOS) to enable reliable native text selection. Replaces SwiftUI Text with .textSelection(.enabled) which is unreliable inside ScrollView on both platforms.

## Scope
- New component: `SelectableText.swift` in Utils/
- Migrate MessageDetailView (ConversationView.swift)
- Migrate CommandOutputDetailView
- Migrate DebugLogsView
- CommandExecutionView excluded (different usage pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Long-press selects text on iOS in MessageDetailView
2. Click-drag selects text on macOS in MessageDetailView
3. Selected text can be copied via system popup (iOS) or Cmd+C (macOS)
4. Text appearance matches current rendering (font, size, color)
5. Monospaced text renders correctly in command output views
6. Small font (10pt) renders correctly in DebugLogsView
7. Parent ScrollView scrolling works correctly (no conflicts)
8. No gray/disabled appearance on either platform
9. Empty text does not crash
10. Very long text renders without performance issues

title: Create SelectableText component
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/selectable-text-component.md#component-api

## Context
This is the foundation task for the SelectableText feature. Creates the new cross-platform component that all view migrations will depend on.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create new file `ios/VoiceCode/Utils/SelectableText.swift`
- [ ] Implement `SelectableText` SwiftUI View wrapper with API:
  - `text: String` (required)
  - `isMonospaced: Bool = false`
  - `fontSize: CGFloat? = nil`
  - `textColor: Color? = nil`

- [ ] Build succeeds on macOS

- No gray/disabled appearance on either platform
- Empty text does not crash

title: macOS Desktop Redesign
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md

## Overview
Transform the macOS app from 'iOS app on Mac' into a native desktop experience. Implements persistent sidebar navigation, push-to-talk voice input, command palette, proper Settings scene, menu bar quick access, and information density adjustments while maintaining voice-first identity.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Sidebar persists while viewing conversation (NavigationSplitView)
2. Push-to-talk works with Option+Space anywhere in app
3. Command palette opens with Cmd+K and supports fuzzy search
4. Settings opens as separate window via Cmd+,
5. Menu bar icon shows connection status and enables quick voice capture
6. Information density is higher than iOS (smaller spacing, more visible content)
7. All keyboard shortcuts documented in menu items
8. iOS functionality remains unchanged (no regressions)
9. Session switching possible without losing conversation context
10. Voice input works seamlessly via both click and keyboard

## Implementation Phases
- Phase 1: Navigation Foundation (NavigationSplitView, SessionSidebarView)
- Phase 2: Settings & Keyboard (Settings scene, .commands{} shortcuts)
- Phase 3: Push-to-Talk (PushToTalkModifier, RecordingIndicator)
- Phase 4: Command Palette (CommandPaletteView)
- Phase 5: Menu Bar Extra (MenuBarContentView, sendQuickPrompt)
- Phase 6: Polish (DesktopDensity, hover states)

title: Add sendQuickPrompt method to VoiceCodeClient
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#data-model-changes

## Context
The menu bar quick capture needs a simplified API to send prompts without managing full session lifecycle. This method creates a temporary session, sends the prompt, and returns the response.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add sendQuickPrompt(text:directory:completion:) method
- [ ] Create or reuse session in specified directory
- [ ] Send prompt via existing WebSocket infrastructure
- [ ] Return first response text via completion handler
- [ ] Handle errors by returning nil
- [ ] Track directory usage via AppSettings.trackDirectoryUsage

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/VoiceCodeClient.swift
- Dependencies: AppSettings.trackDirectoryUsage

Method signature:
```swift
func sendQuickPrompt(text: String, directory: String, completion: @escaping (String?) -> Void)
```

Implementation approach:
1. Find or create session with matching workingDirectory
2. Set up one-time response handler
3. Send prompt message
4. On response, call completion with text
5. On error/timeout, call completion with nil
6. Call settings.trackDirectoryUsage(directory)

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: completion called with response text on success
- [ ] Unit test: completion called with nil on error
- [ ] Integration test: prompt sent and response received

- Files to modify: Various view files to apply modifiers
- Dependencies: None (can be done independently)

View modifiers pattern:
```swift
extension View {
    @ViewBuilder
    func desktopDensity() -> some View {
        #if os(macOS)
        self.font(DesktopDensity.bodyFont)
        #else
        self
        #endif
    }
}
```

Apply to: SessionSidebarRow, MessageView (truncation), list rows

title: Register voicecode:// URL scheme for deep linking

Circle()

title: Implement PushToTalkModifier for Option+Space voice input
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#2-push-to-talk-voice-input

## Context
Desktop users want hands-on-keyboard voice access. This modifier enables push-to-talk via Option+Space, starting recording on key down and stopping on key up.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create PushToTalkModifier.swift with ViewModifier protocol
- [ ] Accept VoiceInputManager as @ObservedObject parameter
- [ ] Track isHolding state to prevent repeated key events
- [ ] Handle .down phase: start recording if not already holding
- [ ] Handle .up phase: stop recording if was holding
- [ ] Ignore events without Option modifier
- [ ] Create View extension: .pushToTalk(voiceInput:)
- [ ] Platform-guard with #if os(macOS)

## Technical Approach
- Files to create: ios/VoiceCode/Utils/PushToTalkModifier.swift
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift
- Dependencies: MacOSRootView should exist for context

Key implementation:
```swift
.onKeyPress(" ", phases: [.down, .up]) { press in
    guard press.modifiers == .option else { return .ignored }
    // Handle down/up phases
}
```

ConversationView integration:
```swift
#if os(macOS)
.pushToTalk(voiceInput: voiceInputManager)
#endif
```

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: Option+Space down calls startRecording(), sets isHolding true
- [ ] Unit test: Option+Space up calls stopRecording(), sets isHolding false
- [ ] Unit test: Space without Option returns .ignored
- [ ] Manual: Hold Option+Space starts recording with visual feedback
- [ ] Manual: Release stops recording and shows transcription

## Acceptance Criteria

- Voice input works seamlessly via both click and keyboard
